opening_query: I am being sued by OneMain for falling behind on a loan during the pandemic and the loan was charged off for delinquency. The amount owed is $4,505.32. I'm currently unemployed with no major assets along with past debts being sent to debt collectors and an active student loan that isn't behind currently. I think the best option is for me to file for bankruptcy but I'm not sure where to start or if that is the best option.

screen.question: What is your main problem right now?
options: Debt collection and lawsuit (OneMain) and debt help
Figuring out bankruptcy as an option

screen.question: Are you looking for help with all of your debts, just your student loans, or both?
Do you have any other debts besides the OneMain loan and the student loan?

opening_query: My landlord is refusing to fix a sewer leak issue, along with standing water under my home that is causing mold, and rodents that were living in my vents. Now she's trying to say it's my fault and I need to pay for everything, even though they are admitting the issues and know what's going on, as they are trying to get the builders the fix the issues. They keep lying to me and now for over 2 weeks I've had terrible sewer leaks they won't fix or even entertain. They said those leaks have fixed themselves all on their own, and they can't be leaking anymore.

screen.question: Are you renting a house or an apartment?
Do you have a written lease with your landlord?
Have you told your landlord about the repairs in writing (for example, in an email or letter)?

screen.question: What is the main issue you need help with first?
options: Licensing and registrations for my consulting business

screen.question: What kind of consulting is it?

opening_query: I am a PA ( Physician Associate/Assistant) I practice in Oregon. I am trying to get legal advise as to weather I can practice at a secondary employer with out a collaborative agreement and with out that employer employing an MD,DO or DMP.

screen.question: Is your main question about Oregon PA licensing rules (what the Oregon Medical Board allows), or about your job/contract with the second employer?

At the second job, will you have any supervising or collaborating clinician (a doctor like an MD/DO, or another licensed prescriber like a nurse practitioner) to work with you under a collaborative agreement (a written plan for supervision/collaboration)?
options: No, nobody like that is involved
Yes, but they are not employed by that workplace
Yes, and they are employed by that workplace

screen.question: For the second job, will you be working as an employee or as an independent contractor (1099 worker)?

opening_query: I sold my RV and did a written contract stating that this person would buy it and pay $1000 a month. They made one payment and took off with my RV. It has been a year and a half and I’ve been trying to figure this out I know they’re back in Oregon so I’m trying to solve this issue as quickly as possible. They owe me $14,000 or my RV back. I have tried to contact him in the past and they refused to have any contact or to resolve this issue. I would really like to talk to an attorney and start the process for civil court case. I need a consultation with an attorney and I need an attorney to help me. Write up paperwork and help me walk me through this process. If you guys could get back to me at the earliest that would be much appreciated. Looking forward to hearing from you.

screen.question: Did you have a written contract for the sale of the RV?
Do you know where the person who owes you money lives in Oregon?
Do you have any proof that the person took your RV and owes you money?
options: Yes, I have a written contract
No, I don't have any proof

opening_query: I and my brother were my Mother's POA's and I am on her banking accounts. I can write checks ect. My mother had dementia and lives alone in her own house. My siblings and I have suggested in home care, meals on wheels ect. My mother has denied all. I went over after calling her and she had moldy food, moldy dishes, her sink was full of putrid water, her car was no working because she had left the door open and drained the battery. On that day she asked if she should live at home anymore. I said no, not anymore. My siblings and I found an apartment in an independent living community, and chose an apartment. We took her to the apartment to see it and she discussed what furnishings and decorating ect. She was hesitant but feeling good my the end of the meal we had there. I have been informed that our POA's were replaced with a "friend of my mothers" and that my siblings and I are accused of elder abuse and stealing from her. I have no idea what to do?

opening_query: On 04/17/2024 we purchased a used 2019 Tiffin Wayfarer RV with 9K miles form Vernon's RV in Happy Hallow, OR. Purchased with understanding this RV was in excellent condition with no issues and we also purchased a 4 year warranty. Drove 600 miles to home and identified multiple issues resulting in repair estimate costs of over $10k. Since purchase, this motorhome has been unusable for over 5 months while parked at repair facilities waiting for repairs. While waiting on repairs we received 3 recall notices from Tiffin advising to not operate the motorhome until these recalls corrected. Current repair facility ordered recall parts and still waiting for repairs. Recalls are searchable on Google and were issued 1-3 years before we purchased from Vernon's. Vernon's stated they searched for recalls and found none before selling to us. Vernon's failed identify recalls that should have been corrected before offering this motorhome for sale. They sold us an safe vehicle.

screen.question: What do you want help with most?
options: Problems with the RV (like hidden defects, safety issues, or recalls)
Problems with the warranty (like a denied claim or delays)
Both the RV problems and the warranty

screen.question: Did you buy the RV in the same state where you live now, or a different state?
Did you pay for the RV with a loan, or did you pay in full?
options: A mix of both (down payment + loan)

There's sexual harassment, discrimination, defamation of character, slander threats an unbelievably hostile work environment wage discrepancies and they switch me from salary one week to hourly wages the next just to avoid paying me my overtime she beats on my door at all hours of the day and night screening text messages that are unbelievable voicemails that are unbelievable there have been employees that have quit because of the way she treats me in front of them and guests so I finally contacted the labor board and they referred me can contact you guys immediately if not sooner please help it's gotten to the point now where I'm afraid to go into work because I don't know what to expect and I don't want to be ridiculed and humiliated and strained after hours
